etymology.description: 𠂇 (pictogram of left hand) + 工 (work; assist) -> original form of 佐 (assist) -> phonetic 𠂇 (left hand) + semantic 工 (work) -> left
etymology.interpretation: The character originally depicted a left hand and later included 工 (work) to form a compound that conveys assisting, which the left hand was traditionally associated with. This developed into a phono-semantic compound where 𠂇 provides the sound 'zuǒ' and 工 adds a context of work or function, maintaining the idea of the left side as supportive or adjunctive.

examples.chinese: 我习惯用左手写字(我習慣用左手寫字)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ xíguàn yòng zuǒ shǒu xiězì.
examples.english: I am accustomed to writing with my left hand.

examples.chinese: 在地图上，左边是北方(在地圖上，左邊是北方)。
examples.pinyin: Zài dìtú shàng, zuǒ biān shì běifāng.
examples.english: On the map, the left side is north.

examples.chinese: 请向左转，然后你会看到公园(請向左轉，然後你會看到公園)。
examples.pinyin: Qǐng xiàng zuǒ zhuǎn, ránhòu nǐ huì kàn dào gōngyuán.
examples.english: Please turn left, and then you will see the park.
